medications.indication_rationale: Soothes the self-induced irritation and supports the skin barrier; stop harsh soaps/sanitizers.
The depigmented skin lacks melanin and burns easily; daily photoprotection is important.
Not indicated — this is not a fungal infection (no scale/active border).
Repigmentation options exist but are a GP/dermatology decision at routine review, not an acute telehealth initiation.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Stop scrubbing/harsh washing, use lukewarm water and a gentle cleanser, apply moisturiser, and use SPF 50+ on the depigmented skin daily.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Lets the reactive redness settle and protects the unpigmented skin.

patient_education_points.point: These are vitiligo patches — a harmless loss of skin pigment, not a fungus, parasite, burn or infection. The skin is structurally healthy and it is not contagious; you can't wash it away.
Stop scrubbing (the red rim is just from that and will fade), wash gently with lukewarm water, moisturise, and use SPF 50+ on the white skin daily because it burns easily.

See your GP/dermatologist in a couple of weeks to discuss options. See a doctor sooner if a patch develops a thick crusty scale, breaks into a bleeding/ulcerated sore, or becomes hot and painful with a fever.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Confirm vitiligo, discuss natural history and treatment options (topical therapy/phototherapy/camouflage).
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Scaly/crusty change, ulceration, or hot painful spreading redness with fever.

must_include_items: Exclude tinea/pityriasis versicolor (scale/active border) and necrosis/infection before reassuring
Reassure benign, non-contagious vitiligo and advise stopping harsh scrubbing/soaps
Advise daily photoprotection of the depigmented skin
errors_of_commission: Empirical antifungal treatment for a non-scaly depigmented lesion
ED over-triage for painless depigmentation as a 'flesh-eating' infection
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: T1 case: passing requires a fungal/necrosis screen + benign reassurance + photoprotection + safety-net. Empirical antifungals or ED over-triage is a failure.
